title: Rapid-response pack template for active disasters
context: In active disaster response (earthquakes, floods, conflict displacement), the mapping need is time-critical and the humanitarian stakes are highest. A rapid-response pack template must differ from the standard template: gates must be completed with urgency without being skipped, and the do-no-harm risk is elevated (displaced/vulnerable populations, conflict contexts, rapidly changing ground truth). This is the urgent:true variant of the pack template, for use only with HOT activation or equivalent expert/ethics authorization. Expert/ethics sign-off is required before any mapping begins.
objective: Design and commit a rapid-response pack template for active disaster contexts, addressing expedited gate review, elevated riskTier:high escalation, and the requirement for HOT activation or equivalent expert/ethics authorization.
acceptanceCriteria: Template is clearly marked as the urgent:true variant; it explicitly requires HOT activation or equivalent expert/ethics authorization before use.
Expedited gate review process is defined: imaging and do-no-harm gates must still be completed but with named reviewers on call and a maximum review turnaround time.
Template escalates riskTier to high for any conflict or displaced-population context and requires expert/ethics sign-off before mapping begins.
Template includes a 'no-map' default clause: if the do-no-harm screen cannot be completed with urgency due to lack of qualified reviewers, mapping is halted.
Template references the standard template (template-004) as its base and documents only the rapid-response differences.
Reviewed by the do-no-harm reviewer and an expert/ethics reviewer.
output: A committed rapid-response pack template document extending template-004 for active disaster contexts, with expedited gate review procedures, high-risk escalation rules, HOT activation requirement, and a no-map default.
